News.title: Fiend who drugged unconscious date to rape him multiple times before he died in chem sex horror is jailed
News.content:
A DEPRAVED fiend who drugged his date to rape him multiple times before he died has been jailed.

Derek Rowley’s victim became unwell and fell unconscious after “chem sex” at his Kent home.





The pair had connected online and started taking drugs including crystal meth, ketamine and GHB on the afternoon of May 21, 2024.

They began having consensual sex, while on livestream, but at around 5pm the 61-year-old victim became unresponsive.

Instead of calling paramedics, Rowley, 45, was seen injecting a substance into the victim’s mouth and repeatedly raping him.

Maidstone Crown Court heard how the twisted fiend gave a “thumbs up” to people viewing the steam.



At around 9.40pm, the vile rapist went downstairs to tell relatives someone had died in his room.

Emergency services were called to the property, in Gillingham, at around 9.50pm and the 61-year-old victim was pronounced dead at the scene.

Maidstone Crown Court heard the victim had died at around 7.30pm from mixed drugs toxicity.

An impact statement read to the court on behalf of the victim’s partner described him as “gentle, empathetic, he was one of the good guys” and how now there is a “massive gaping hole left in my heart”.



He said: “I feel broken and shattered by it all, I don’t really sleep, still feel like I’m in survival mode and I’m stuck.”

The victim’s family members also said the knowledge of his suffering is “unbearable and stays with us every day”.

They said his abuse was “entirely preventable” and what happened to him involved “reckless behaviour” that stripped him of dignity.

Judge Robert Lazarus said Rowley had “complete disregard for his (the victim’s) wellbeing”.

He added: “(It) needs to be said… that anybody with a sense of humanity would have called an ambulance as soon as (the victim) fell into a coma and not proceed to rape him repeatedly over a period of one hour and 20 minutes.



“You wanted to drug (the victim) to the extent of putting him into a coma and once you did that you wanted to rape him multiple times while livestreaming this.”

Rowley was found guilty of 11 counts of rape and one count of administering a substance with intent following a trial in November last year.

He was today sentenced today to 17 years in jail.

Investigating officer Detective Inspector Lee Neiles said: “This was a truly shocking and disturbing case.

“Rowley exploited the victim in the most appalling way, showing complete disregard for his welfare.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Thanks to the meticulous work of our investigation team and the evidence presented to the jury, Rowley has been held accountable for his actions and will now face a consideration amount of time behind bars.

“Our thoughts remain with the victim’s family during this incredibly difficult time who have shown tremendous dignity throughout the investigation and trial.”

News.title: Boy, 15, seriously injured in lunchtime stabbing at UK school – as pupil arrested and classrooms locked down
News.content:
SCHOOL HORROR Boy, 15, seriously injured in lunchtime stabbing at UK school – as pupil arrested and classrooms locked down

A 15-YEAR-old boy has been seriously injured after being stabbed at school during lunchtime.

The horror unfolded at Nottingham University Academy of Science and Technology (NUAST) in the Dunkirk area of the city.





Police were called to the scene at around 1.36pm today and the teen was rushed to Queen’s Medical Centre.

A 15-year-old boy was arrested at the scene and it is understood the teens are known to each other.

Nottingham Police also confirmed there is no evidence to suggest the incident was related to the national ‘School Wars’ social posts.

Superintendent Chris Pearson said: “This is obviously an unsettling incident to occur on school grounds, and we are working closely with NUAST.

“A 15-year-old boy has sustained a stab injury and been sent to QMC for treatment. His injuries are currently believed to be serious.

“His family will be supported by specially trained officers while a thorough investigation is underway. A 15-year-old boy has been arrested in connection with this incident.

“We know this is likely to cause alarm and distress across our local community especially for parents and pupils in the area.

“What we know so far is that both victim and suspect are known to each other and there is no information to say this is linked to the national ‘School Wars’ social posts.



“I also want to stress that there is no wider threat to any other pupils at the school. We will be working closely with the school over the coming days to establish what has happened.”

In an email to parents, Dave Thompson, the school’s headteacher, said: “I am very sorry to inform you there was a serious incident at school today in which a student was stabbed.

“The incident in the school is now over and is being dealt with by police.

“We will be open tomorrow as normal.

“Clearly, this has been a very traumatic event for the whole school community and support will be provided for any staff or pupils affected.



“Our thoughts and best wishes are with our students and their families at this incredibly difficult time.”

News.title: Paedo mayor’s ex-husband battled to keep their children away from her ‘abusive’ lover – before he wound up dead
News.content:
TWISTED PAST Paedo mayor’s ex-husband battled to keep their children away from her ‘abusive’ lover – before he wound up dead

THE ex-husband of paedo mayor Misty Roberts fought to keep the couple’s children away from her allegedly abusive lover.

After a bitter battle, the paedo mayor‘s lover, William Andrew Cronce, 38, wound up dead.





Misty Roberts, 43, could face up to 17 years behind bars when she is sentenced next month for sexually abusing a 16-year-old at a boozy pool party.

The former Louisiana mayor had sex with her son’s teenage friend and tried to spark a fight between the boys to dodge the blame, a jury found.

Now, chilling court records reviewed by the New York Post, have uncovered a new twist in the tale.

Roberts’ ex-husband, Duncan Clanton, filed a restraining order against the mayor’s lover Cronce, documents reveal.

Clanton was desperate to keep his kids, then 10 and 13, away from pipeliner Cronce, who he claimed had alcoholic, suicidal and “stalky” tendencies.

He begged a Beauregard Parish court judge for a restraining order against Cronce in a letter dated 24 January, 2024.

Clanton wrote: “I am fearful for my children’s safety because William Andrew Cronce does have access to weapons/handguns, he is bold enough to publicly voice/threaten suicide and has shown stalking behavior with the mother of my children.”



He said Cronce suffered “instability”, and that in one instance, the pipeliner had “exited the house by loudly shouting in front of [the kids] that their Mother needs to learn to shut her ‘c—k sucker’, if she knows what’s good for her”.

The ex-husband alleged that Cronce had posted on social media threatening to take his own life – and claimed he was later found by cops “incapacitated and unconscious with a gun on his person”.

He wrote that the pipeliner: “Has a history of instability and alcoholism that is abusive in relationships. Our children told me this is specifically the reason why [his and Roberts] relationship ended, because their Mother didn’t want them to be raised in a household that way.

“Now, they are back together and our children, in my opinion, are forced to witness a toxic relationship.”

On 2 February 2024, Cronce’s lawyer responded to the request for a restraining order, denying abuse allegations.



But just three weeks later, Cronce was dead.

His cause of death remains a mystery.

It comes after paedo mayor Roberts was found guilty of indecent behaviour and carnal knowledge of a minor.

She faces up to 17 years in prison if the judge imposes the longest terms for both counts.

They took less than an hour to render the guilty verdict.



A jury heard Roberts was heavily intoxicated, had taken a photo with the teenager and was flirting with him during the party on 20 July, 2024.

They then disappeared upstairs, and Roberts’ son and nephew snuck up and saw them having sex, afterwards realising the “weight of the situation” and began talking about how to stop it.

Shocking testimony from the victim laid bare the chaotic fallout after the disgraced divorcee was discovered in the bed with the boy.

The teen told the court a “big argument” erupted, during which the mum-of two tried to spark a fight.

Roberts’ furious son confronted the pair, claiming he had even recorded the act in an upstairs “bonus room”.



But instead of taking responsibility, Roberts allegedly tried to shift the blame onto the boy.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Pointing at the teen, she yelled at her son: “Go fight your friend. He’s the one who did it!”

The once-influential mayor of DeRidder, a town of nearly 10,000 people, will be required to register as a sex offender.

News.title: Vile tourist tortures flamingo as shocking CCTV shows him dragging bird by neck to his Las Vegas hotel room
News.content:
A CRUEL tourist has been slapped with animal abuse charges after he allegedly stole and tortured a flamingo.

Mitchell Fairbarn, 33, is facing four counts of felony animal abuse after he stole the bird from its enclosure at an iconic Strip hotel in Las Vegas.





Police say they responded to a call from the Flamingo Hotel on Tuesday, where the Ontario man allegedly trespassed on the property and stole the animal, before taking it back to his hotel room and torturing it.

He has also been accused of harming several other birds when he allegedly broke into the property’s Flamingo Wildlife Habitat.

Investigators who reviewed the hotel CCTV footage said it showed Fairbarn entering the enclosure at 5am before grabbing a Chilean flamingo named Peachy.



While attempting to steal Peachy from his enclosure, the vile holidaymaker allegedly injured other animals, including “pinning down” a second bird and not allowing it to escape his clutches.

READ MORE WORLD NEWS THEME PARK HORROR Man stabbed at Flamingo Land Resort theme park as cops arrest 4

Further surveillance footage shows Fairbarn exit a hotel elevator with the bird.

He had allegedly used his own shirt to wrap the animal up, before wrangling it by the neck and legs in the corridor as he walked back to his room.

Police say he later admitted to trespassing into the habitat after seeing the bird in distress.



Fairbarn allegedly claimed he had “popped” the animal’s wing into place.

In a statement, police said he had claimed to be a “farm boy”.

“He stated he knows that ‘popping’ the wing back into its place is a common practice for birds, such as ducks,” the statement said.

Despite this, cops say they found multiple videos on Fairbarn’s phone, allegedly showing him choking the flamingo and throwing it onto the floor.

In one clip, the depraved accused bird-napper laughed while declaring that he was “taking [the bird] home”.



Police documents say Peachy cried out while he was being choked, according to the Las Vegas Review-Journal.

The arrest report said he had “absolutely no regard for safety and wellness of the animals”, according to the Daily Star.

Police later found “a large bloody feather” along with other material inside Fairbarn’s hotel room.

Peachy has since been rescued from Fairbarn’s grip.

According to animal control, he sustained a wing injury from his wing being “pulled” from his body.



The flamboyant bird needed stitches, while two other flamingos that were injured in Fairbarn’s alleged rampage through the habitat also required treatment.

Authorities say he injured Peachy’s wing and ruptured its blood supply during the incident, saying he “willfully and maliciously severely injured a federally protected migratory species” such as the flamingo.”

He was subsequently arrested on animal torture charges and currently faces four felony counts of torturing, maiming or killing an animal kept for companionship or pleasure.

Fairbarn spent Wednesday night in police custody, and later posted bail.

He has been ordered to stay away from the Las Vegas Strip after posting a $12,000 (£9,000) bail on Thursday, according to 8 News Now.



Caesars Entertainment Corp – which owns the Flamingo Hotel – issued a statement on Wednesday, calling the incident “deeply distressing”.

“Our beloved birds, including Peachy, who was cruelly taken from the habitat, are currently in the care of dedicated veterinarians and our extraordinary Flamingo animal care team,” the statement read.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We are hopeful they will make a full recovery.”

Fairbarn will return to court on March 9.

News.title: Iran blitzes Bahrain’s biggest petrol refinery as missiles blast sparks huge inferno – spiking prices worldwide
News.content:
IRAN has blown up Bahrain’s biggest petrol refinery, sending global markets haywire.

The primary refinery for the Bahrain Petroleum Company in Ma’ameer erupted into flames after it was struck by an Iranian ballistic missile.





Footage from the region showed two massive columns of smoke rising into the sky.

At the base of one plume, a large fire was visibly raging.

Bahrain’s Ministry of the Interior confirmed the attack on social media.

“One facility in Maameer was targeted, and the relevant authorities are handling the incident,” it said in a post.

Read more in World News DON'S DECISION Trump rules out dead Ayatollah's 'lightweight' son as next Iran leader

The Ministry said flames have since been brought under control and that nobody was injured.

“The fire broke out in one of the facilities in Ma’ameer, which was targeted by the Iranian aggression, has been brought under control,” it said.

“Limited material damage was reported, with no loss of life.”



Ma’ameer is home to the Bahrain Petroleum Company refinery, as well as other factories that produce various materials such as concrete and asphalt.

The refinery – run by Bahrain’s state oil company Bapco – is located on Bahrain’s east coast, south of the capital Manama.

It has a maximum production capacity of 267,000 barrels of oil per day and storage capacity for up to 14 million barrels.

Bahrain is one of several Gulf countries to be targeted by Iran in a bombing blitz.

The Islamic Republic is carrying out a campaign of retaliation after joint US-Israeli strikes took out Ayatollah Khamenei on Saturday.



Tehran has responded with retaliatory attacks across US bases in the Middle East.

It comes as…

Britain has already announced it will send the destroyer HMS Dragon to the region, although the warship won’t depart Portsmouth until next week.

The first UK evacuation flight for Brits stranded in the region has left Oman, with a further seven flights due to leave the UAE today.

Militants from several Kurdish Iranian factions reportedly launched a ground offensive last night in a bid to topple Iran’s mullahs.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

The militias are backed by Mossad and the CIA, US and Israeli officials told Axios.

Secretary of War Pete Hegseth has declared Iran “toast” and stated the US is “winning decisively” in “Operation Epic Fury”.

News.title: Trump rules out dead Ayatollah’s ‘lightweight’ son as next Iran leader as Don says HE will pick so there’s no more war
News.content:
DONALD Trump has ruled out the slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s son as Iran’s next Supreme Leader – branding him a “lightweight”.

The US president vowed to hand-pick the regime’s new ruler amid mounting speculation that Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, will replace his late father.





Trump told Axios on Thursday he needed to be personally involved in the selection process – the same way he did in Venezuela after the capture of Nicolas Maduro.

President Trump said: “They are wasting their time. Khamenei’s son is a lightweight. I have to be involved in the appointment, like with Delcy [Rodriguez] in Venezuela.”

Without his involvement, the US would be back to war with Iran “in five years”, Trump added.

“Khamenei’s son is unacceptable to me. We want someone that will bring harmony and peace to Iran,” the US leader said.



It comes after Mojtaba, the late Ayatollah’s son, was tipped to be Iran’s next ruler following the assassination of Khamenei, 86, on Saturday.

Trump also said on Tuesday that most candidates Washington had in mind to replace Khamenei had been killed.

The White House has denied claims that the main objective of the US-Israeli strikes is regime change – instead insisting they are targeting Iranian nuclear capabilities.

Mojtaba was selected as the primary candidate to succeed his dad following two Assembly of Experts meetings in Iran, regime officials told The New York Times.



He is understood to be a hardline conservative cleric who advocated for developing nuclear bombs – and who has strong links to Iran’s brutal Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).

The late tyrant’s son, who reportedly exercised large amounts of influence while his dad was still in power, has now been put in Israel’s cross hairs.

The Israeli military has vowed to “eliminate” whoever is appointed as the regime’s next ruler.

Mojtaba owns luxury pads in the UK worth more than £100million, including 11 homes in Bishop’s Avenue – nicknamed Billionaire’s Row – according to Bloomberg.

He is believed to control a sprawling investment empire across Tehran, Dubai and Frankfurt – and has reportedly stashed cash away in Swiss bank accounts.



The radical cleric was also reportedly treated for impotency at hospitals in London.

He was once under pressure from his family to produce heirs, according to a classified 2008 briefing sent from the State Department to the US embassy in London.

He reportedly needed four visits hospital, including one final stay which lasted two months – and eventually had a son named after his father Ali.



US intelligence also suggests Mojtaba married late in life in 2004 – reportedly due to the “impotency problem treated and eventually resolved during three extended visits to the UK”.

The former Ayatollah’s son visited Wellington and Cromwell Hospitals in London, the intelligence says.

The report reads: “Mojtaba was expected by his family to produce children quickly, but needed a fourth visit to the UK for medical treatment.

“After a stay of two months, his wife became pregnant. Back in Iran, a healthy boy was born, named Ali for his paternal grandfather.”

It comes after Sir Keir Starmer announced Britain will send four more RAF Typhoon fighter jets to the Middle East as he fought back against criticism over his response to the Iran war.



The PM announced four extra fighters will deploy to Qatar to bolster Britain’s response as the conflict spreads across the region.

It comes after Sir Keir faced fierce criticism for initially blocking Donald Trump from using British bases for bombing runs on Iran.

He later reversed course and allowed the US to launch “limited defensive” missions from Diego Garcia.



Speaking in a press conference on Thursday, Sir Keir insisted his decision not to join the initial US and Israeli strikes was “deliberate” and in the national interest.

Israel is also set to attack Iran’s underground missile tunnel in its second phase of war, sources say.

Iran’s sprawling “missile city” packed with suicide drones and deadly rockets will be hit, in a bid to neutralise Iran’s ability to launch aerial attacks at Israel.

Iran’s bloodthirsty terror chiefs & exiled rivals battle for power as regime collapses By Katie Davis IRAN’S merciless regime is on the brink of collapse – with terror chiefs and rival opposition figures vying for control. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei’s death has thrown the Islamic Republic’s future into turmoil, exposing the first legitimate opening to pivot away from repressive rule in decades. After wiping out the Ayatollah and dozens of top commanders in Saturday’s mammoth Epic Fury blitz, Donald Trump issued a rallying cry to Iranians to seize the opportunity to topple the regime for good. “This is the single greatest chance for the Iranian people to take back their country,” the US president said. “Hopefully, the IRGC and police will peacefully merge with the Iranian patriots, and work together as a unit to bring back the country to the greatness it deserves.” Following Khamenei’s death, a shady web of stooges he created in a bid to make his regime “coup-proof” – which The Sun this week revealed – will be stepping up to take the reins. Ayatollah Alireza Arafi has been anointed as an interim leader to pull the strings of terror that Khamenei no longer can. The mullah, president Masoud Pezeshkian and chief justice Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje’i will work to collectively exercise the Supreme Leader’s power until a permanent successor is selected. Khamenei’s second-eldest son Mojtaba, who effectively served as a “mini-Supreme leader” within his father’s office, was tipped to be his most likely heir. Despite being a behind-the-scenes figure, Mojtaba, 56, has long been an influential actor in Iran’s ruling mechanism – and has close ties with the IRGC. Kasra Aarbi, director of IRGC research at United Against Nuclear Iran (UANI), told The Sun Mojtaba will be in the running – providing he’s hasn’t been wiped out. “We haven’t had the reports yet as to whether he is alive,” Kasra said. “But there is an invisible power structure there, an invisible state, that is really calling the shots. His sons are part of that. “It has been designed alongside the IRGC to fill any vacuum and make sure continuity prevails rather than anything that could have a devastating blow to the regime. “Keep an eye on Ayatollah Alireza Arafi. Keep an eye on his sons.”

The Israeli-US joint air assault in Iran is nearing the end of its first week after opening salvos killed the country’s leaders and set off a regional war.

Israel’s operation has expanded to Lebanon, where massive queues are streaming out of Beirut’s southern suburbs – recognised as Hezbollah strongholds – after an unprecedented evacuation warning.



Tehran has responded with retaliatory attacks across US bases in the Middle East.

A series of security alerts rung out at RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus on Thursday, warning locals to take cover just days after the base was blitzed by Iran.

Several European countries have deployed naval assets to the eastern Mediterranean to help protect Cyprus.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

On Thursday, Iran also blitzed Bahrain’s biggest petrol refinery, sending global markets haywire.

News.title: Donald Trump boasts Cuba government 'is about to fall' and it is all his doing
News.content:
Donald Trump boasts Cuba government 'is about to fall' and it is all his doing

Donald Trump has gleefully predicted the downfall of another regime, saying the country is "going to fall" thanks to him.

He said Cuba was on the edge, adding: "We cut off all oil, all money, or we cut off everything coming in from Venezuela, which was the sole source. And they want to make a deal.”

Asked whether the United States was playing a role in the Cuban government’s demise, Trump said: “Well, what do you think? For 50 years, that’s icing on the cake. Venezuela is doing fantastically. [Delcy Rodríguez] is doing a fantastic job. The relationship with them is great.”

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Trump said the United States is in touch with Cuba’s government as instability on the island intensifies with blackouts leaving millions of Cuban's in darkness.

The western half of Cuba was left in darkness after power cuts hit the island nation yesterday, leaving millions of people in Havana and beyond without power as the country struggles with dwindling oil reserves.

It is the second such outage to affect Cuba's western region in the past three months. A huge power cut affected the nation after the national grid collapsed in December.

Trump he suggested the island’s worsening situation is partly the result of U.S. pressure, including cutting off the Venezuelan oil supplies that once kept the lights on in Havana.

“Well, it’s because of my intervention, intervention that is happening,” Trump told Politico. “Obviously, otherwise they wouldn’t have this problem. We cut off all oil, all money, … everything coming in from Venezuela, which was the sole source.”

News.title: Defence Secretary John Healey refuses to rule out British forces getting drawn into Iran war
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Defence Secretary John Healey refuses to rule out British forces getting drawn into Iran war

Defence Secretary John Healey spoke to the Daily Mirror during a quick visit to RAF Akrotiri, Cyprus which was hit by an Iran-backed terror drone as war got underway

EXCLUSIVE: Sirens heard at UK’s RAF Akrotiri base as Defence Secretary visits

British Defence Secretary John Healey has refused to dismiss concerns UK forces will be further drawn into the US and Israel - Iran war.

‌



It came as he was forced to take cover as another alert went off during a visit to RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus and he was twice made to shelter from possible ‘incoming.’ Asked to address worries in the UK about UK involvement and a possible heightened terror threat he said: “I certainly won’t speculate about situations that we might see.

‌



“It is the nature of conflict that you never know what might develop and we are hugely strengthening our defences.”

‌



Mr Healey spoke to the Mirror during a quick visit to RAF Akrotiri, Cyprus which was hit by an Iran-backed terror drone as war got underway. On Thursday RAF Akrotiri was sent into full alert mode as air raid sirens went off a worrying three times - twice during Healey’s visit.

Are you a resident or traveller caught up in the chaos of the Middle East war? Contact us at webnews@mirror.co.uk

View 7 Images UK Defence Secretary John Healey visits RAF Akrotiri (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Mum's terror as kids given one minute to hide from horror missile on RAF base READ MORE :

He said he was about to attend a COBRA meeting online when the alert went off at Akrotiri. He explained: “I was already in a windowless room getting an operational briefing ahead of that meeting and the air raid signal went off.

“It’s a reminder of the increasing and the increasingly indiscriminate threat that Iran poses as we have seen with their first retaliatory poses.”

View 7 Images John Healey meets an F-35 pilot who shot down a drone (Image: Getty Images)

‌



One of the problems for Akrotiri is that it is protected but it is difficult to pick up small incoming drones that dodge radar, even though not by design. And being an island the incoming is low flying over water which adds to the challenge of detecting small objects.

View 7 Images John Healey holds a meeting with Minister of Defence of Cyprus Vasilis Palmas at the Ministry of Defence in Cyprus (Image: Getty Images)

Mr Healey said more forces are on their way as Akrotiri becomes the focus of efforts to protect UK forces from spillover attacks by Iran. He told the Mirror that "specialist air defence planners" have arrived at RAF Akrotiri in the days following the attack by pro Iranian militia in Lebanon or Iraq.

‌



View 7 Images The Defence Secretary's visit to Cyprus comes days after RAF Akrotiri, a British airbase on Cyprus's southern coast, was targeted by Iranian drones (Image: Getty Images)

Investigators are still, according to sources, unsure which fired the drone which hit an Akrotiri aircraft hangar. It has become increasingly alarming because smaller drones sent by Hezbollah or Iraqi militia loyal to the Iranian regime are difficult to track.

View 7 Images The Minister of Defence of Cyprus Vasilis Palmas (R) greets the UK Defence Secretary John Healey (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Another 400 troops, some specialists in monitoring the air for incoming weapons, have arrived in recent days, with the latest addition arriving on Thursday. The Mirror demanded from Mr Healey that he send a message of comfort or encouragement to people in the UK alarmed by the onset of another Middle Eastern conflict.

View 7 Images A U-2 spy plane lands at the UK's RAF Akrotiri air base (Image: AP)

He nodded and answered: “I really get the concerns of folks at home. This is my concern as well as protecting the lives of British military personnel in places like her in Cyprus and also the island of Cyprus.

Article continues below

View 7 Images Daily Mirror's Defence Editor Chris Hughes in Cyprus standing in front of a typhoon and F-35B

“We have strengthened it with extra forces in terms of size. There are 400 more personnel here now and they are defending more than just this island as they help protect British lives at other locations in the Middle East.

“We will be doing all that we can to protect people from these indiscriminate attacks that we are seeing from Iran.”

News.title: 'I visited Cyprus RAF base when air-raid siren forced us to flee at breakneck speed'
News.content:
'I visited Cyprus RAF base when air-raid siren forced us to flee at breakneck speed'

"Within hours we were driven at breakneck speed to safety as a third air-raid siren went up around RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus, sending troops and visitors once again diving for cover"

EXCLUSIVE: Sirens heard at UK’s RAF Akrotiri base as Defence Secretary visits

Minutes before our arrival at RAF Akrotiri, air-raid sirens went off and thousands of British troops dived for cover.

‌



Another alert had gone up - the second in hours- as Akrotiri fast becomes the emergency focus for UK forces and the Iran crisis spirals out of control. And within hours we were driven at breakneck speed to safety as a third air-raid siren went up, sending troops and visitors once again diving for cover.

‌



The eerie whining of WWII-style sirens blaring out the “take cover” warning made troops don helmets and head for shelter on the ground or find nearby bunkers. This was the second time in one day visiting that Defence Secretary John Healey was forced to take cover from an attack threat of incoming missiles or drones.

‌



His cortege whisked him off to safety as he left a meeting with troops converging around fighter jets on standby to protect the base. Minutes later the all-clear signal was delivered as it turned out yet again the warning was either a dud or a threat had not reached Akrotiri.

Are you a resident or traveller caught up in the chaos of the Middle East war? Contact us at webnews@mirror.co.uk

View 7 Images Daily Mirror's Defence Editor Chris Hughes in Cyprus standing in front of a typhoon and F-35B

‌



7 things Keir Starmer announced in his live TV address to nation on Iran crisis READ MORE :

Should Brits pay for their flights back from Dubai? Take our poll and have your say READ MORE :

It was supposed to be alarms such as this that meant Akrotiri has been bolstered for weeks against the threat of war in the Middle East spreading. There are now six F-35B UK fighter jets on permanent standby here, along with eight Typhoon warplanes on high-readiness to thwart an attack.

Within moments of an attack alert they can take to the skies, screaming into position and hunting down missiles or drones trying to kill UK troops. Once they have found them they can blast them to bits with their sophisticated array of high-tech weaponry.

View 7 Images UK Defence Secretary John Healey meets an F-35 pilot who shot down a drone (Image: Getty Images)

‌



The F-35B, a STOVL warplane, or Short Take Off Vertical Landing, warplane is one the deadliest in the world takes off quickly and lands vertically like a helicopter. A killer stealth fighter the F35B Lightning has a 25mm cannon and precision weapons such as the AIM-120 AMRAAM, AIM-132 ASRAAM, and Meteor air-to-air missiles.

The sprawling east Mediterranean island base is the highest concentration of UK forces and hardware in the closest proximity to the war in Iran. There are 4,000 members of British forces here, plus an array of visiting foreign troops.

View 7 Images John Healey visits RAF Akrotiri (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Another four Typhoon fighter jets will arrive on Akrotiri imminently, the government has announced. Wildcat Helicopters will arrive within days and are able to shoot down drones or missiles, whilst Type 45 destroyer HMS Dragon is also en route for the region. The Wildcats are fitted with breathtakingly fine maritime radar systems which can pick up small targets moving through the air - such as the drone that made it through on Sunday.

On Sunday night a small terror drone made it through defences and blew a hole in a large aircraft hangar - but it could have been devastating. It is now known that it either came from Lebanese Hezbollah or pro-Iranian militia in Iraq - and that UK forces are now being targeted.

View 7 Images A U-2 spy aircraft lands at the UK's RAF Akrotiri air base (Image: AP)

‌



Terror groups and even Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps are aware of the UK base’s significance and it is right in the line of fire.

One senior source on the base told me: "The drone was small but deadly. If it had hit a room full of people you would be looking at a room full of casualties.

View 7 Images Minister of Defence of Cyrpus Vasilis Palmas (R) greets the UK Defence Secretary John Healey (Image: Getty Images)

‌



"Our attention is very focused on the threat and there is a state of alert here over the crisis in Iran. The effect of this war has reached us.

"But we have planned for this for some time and made preparations. This place is on a trajectory behind Israel and these missiles are going east to west - naturally there is concern the base could be hit.

View 7 Images John Healey holds a meeting with Minister of Defence of Cyprus Vasilis Palmas at the Ministry of Defence in Cyprus (Image: Getty Images)

‌



"It is early days and there are a lot of countries affected by this crisis so if a missile comes we are all firing at it, to be honest. Hopefully in the future this will become more coordinated."

British forces hope an overarching ‘command and control’ system will be imposed on the multi-force presence in Cyprus - preventing overshoot or many forces firing at one target.

View 7 Images John Healey speaking a pilot at the British airbase on Cyprus's southern coast (Image: Getty Images)

Article continues below

The Mirror spent a day on RAF Akrotiri where more than 1,000 civilians, families of our troops based there have been evacuated. There is a quiet, subdued atmosphere of professionalism within RAF Akrotiri where RAF Typhoon and F35B fighter jets crews are on standby for incoming.

They are on the very frontline in combating the missiles of drones of Iran and its proxy forces that have struck terror across the region. Playing fields, football pitches often playing host to families have fallen silent until the crisis is over and shops have closed down as the owners are terrified.

News.title: Rochdale shooting LIVE: Air ambulance scrambled after life-changing injuries
News.content:
17:56 KEY EVENT

Full police statement

"More information will be provided once we are able to do so."

News.title: Man and teen arrested after viral 'Red vs Blue' school wars trend sparking knife crime panic
News.content:
Man and teen arrested after viral 'Red vs Blue' school wars trend sparking knife crime panic

The Met Police said it arrested a man in his 20s and 15-year-old after a distressing 'school wars' trend called for pupils to fight one another at a couple of schools in London

View 4 Images Police have arrested two people after a distressing trend gripped the internet (Image: Getty Images/iStockphoto)

A man and a teenage boy have been arrested over a viral "Red vs Blue" social media trend that allegedly encouraged pupils to engage in violence with each other.

The Metropolitan Police said a man in his 20s and a 15-year-old boy were arrested in Central East London at around 6.45pm on March 1 on suspicion of encouraging or assisting in a crime. Both have been remanded in custody, and police said the arrests related to a recent viral social media trend in which pupils were allegedly encouraged to fight one another in "school wars".

The trend had led London schools to issue warnings and police to increase patrols, after posts reportedly referenced bringing items like compasses and metal combs to the would-be battles.

Inside the 'vile' Red vs Blue School Wars craze sparking knife crime panic in UK READ MORE :

School issues warning over disturbing social media craze targeting children READ MORE :

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 4 Images School Wars' is a social media craze that allegedly pits pupils from different schools against one another, with points earned for attacking students from other schools

The trend, dubbed "Red vs Blue" online, gathered momentum on TikTok and Snapchat and proposed fights between rival schools meeting up after lessons. The schools were divided into colour coded teams, and some posts had advised people taking part to arm themselves with sharp objects.

Posts and viral videos had urged violence in the name of scoring "points" against their peers after they started hitting social media around February this year.

In one post, four schools were named and pitted against one another in red and blue teams in a "North London war". In another "Hackney war", children were told to "bring your best weapons", with a picture of scissors, a school ruler, knife, compass, comb and fireworks.

View 4 Images The social media trend had encouraged children to 'be violent' (Image: Instagram)

Among the boroughs mentioned in the posts were Hackney, Islington, Redbridge and Croydon, including 26 schools in total across the two teams.

In response, police issued Section 35 disposal orders, allowing officers to remove individuals from a set area to prevent crime, antisocial behaviour, or other disorder. While the posts don't appear to have prompted any real-life fighting as of yet, they did cause limited local panic, with parents in some areas advised to collect their children directly from the school gates as a precaution.

Much of the fomenting chaos appears to have come from fake content, with early posts accelerating the trend showing two men wearing red and blue balaclavas now believed to have been AI-generated.

View 4 Images The Met Police said it has boosted its presence outside schools due to the trend (Image: PAUL GILLIS / Reach Plc)

A spokesperson for the Met Police said in a statement today that a "strong police presence" has been posted outside local schools around London.

They said: "On Sunday, 1 March at approximately 18:45hrs, Met officers arrested two individuals in Central East London in connection with a recent trend of social media posts. Both suspects, a man aged in his 20s and a boy aged 15, were arrested on suspicion of encouraging or assisting in a crime.

Article continues below

“They remain in police custody. A strong police presence remains around schools, particularly at the end of the school day. Local officers continue to work alongside local authority partners, school leaders and parents, whose joint robust response has been vital in protecting young people across the last week.”

News.title: Donald Trump slams Iran's 'new Supreme Leader' as 'lightweight' and demands he chooses
News.content:
Donald Trump has said he needs to be personally involved in selecting Iran's next leader - and has ripped into the man expected to be chosen.

Trump made it clear he found it unacceptable that Mojtaba Khamenei, son of assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei, would be the next supreme leader.

"They are wasting their time. Khamenei's son is a lightweight. I have to be involved in the appointment, like with Delcy [Rodriguez] in Venezuela," Trump told Axios.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Trump's outburst comes after Iranian officials suggested they were "close" to announcing Khamenei's successor. His son Mojtaba is widely thought to be the frontrunner.

Image: AFP via Getty Images) AFP via Getty Images)

The president made clear that he would not accept a new Iranian leader who would continue Khamenei's policies, which he said would force the US back to war "in five years".

"Khamenei's son is unacceptable to me. We want someone that will bring harmony and peace to Iran," Trump said. The statement indicates Trump wants the US to have an extraordinary amount of political power over Iran in future, as the objectives of the massive US-Israeli assault remain unclear.

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and other US officials have denied that the goal of the operation is "regime change", focusing instead on degrading Iran's missile capabilities, nuclear program and Navy.

Mojtaba Khamenei, the late Ayatollah's second-oldest son, is 56 years old. Iran's leading figures are reportedly preparing to name him as the next Supreme Leader of the faltering nation.

Image: NurPhoto via Getty Images) NurPhoto via Getty Images)

Donald Trump previously said the regime had slim pickings for a new leader as many of the top candidates had been killed. He added: "I guess the worst case would be we do this and somebody takes over who’s as bad as the previous person. We don’t want that to happen."

The US President said he wanted "someone from within" Iran's government to take power once the bombing campaign began. Asked on Tuesday who could replace Khamenei, Trump told reporters at the White House: "Most of the people we had in mind are dead."

News.title: Inside Britney Spears' traumatic spiral with 'real reason' for devastating downfall
News.content:
Inside Britney Spears' traumatic spiral with 'real reason' for devastating downfall

As new information emerges regarding Britney Spears's arrest last night, The Mirror takes a look at the pop icon's devastating spiral back in the mid-'00s, with her breakdown captured in heartbreaking footage

View 8 Images Britney Spears, who has now been arrested, faced a rough time in the '00s

Concerns have grown for Britney Spears, who was arrested last night for Driving Under the Influence, TMZ claims, in what's been described as an emotional incident.

‌



It's also believed the pop icon was booked early this morning (March 5) at approximately 3 am, after being handcuffed by California Highway Patrol at around 9:30 pm the previous evening. It's understood Britney, 44, was released at around 6 am this morning, as per Ventura County Sheriff's records, and is now scheduled to appear in court on May 4.

‌



While Britney has yet to publicly comment on these reports, a representative for the legendary singer has released a statement asserting that "she needs help". They previously told The Mirror: "This was an unfortunate incident that is completely inexcusable. Britney is going to take the right steps and comply with the law and hopefully this can be the first step in long overdue change that needs to occur in Britney’s life. Hopefully, she can get the help and support she needs during this difficult time. Her boys are going to be spending time with her. Her loved ones are going to come up with an overdue needed plan to set her up for success for well being."

‌



While Britney has in many ways lived a charmed life, filled with success and accomplishments many could only dream of, she's also faced a number of personal difficulties. This became particularly apparant in 2007, when the much loved star suffered a well documented meltdown at the height of her fame.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 8 Images Britney shot to fame at a young age, and her talent was evident (Image: Instagram/britneyspears)

‌



With her trademark pigtails and innocent charm, Britney shot to stardom with her debut single ...Baby One More Time in 1998. For four years, she dominated the charts, racking up hit after hit, earning millions from global tours and securing the title of world's most powerful celebrity by Forbes magazine. But cracks began to appear in December 2002, when her three-year relationship with Justin Timberlake ended.

Public opinion seemed to shift, and the following August, she faced hostile jeers from fans during a world tour and faced backlash for kissing Madonna on stage at the MTV Video Music Awards. Her parents split, and she impulsively eloped to Las Vegas to wed childhood friend Jason Alexander, only for the marriage to be annulled a mere 55 hours later.

Back in 2019, a Channel 5 documentary, Britney Spears-Breaking Point, examined how the Louisiana-born star's pursuit of normality led her to marry backing dancer Kevin Federline just eight months later - a relationship that would trigger her downfall. "Britney was desperate for love. From the earliest age, Britney wanted everybody to love her, including Kevin," Rob Shuter, former executive editor of US OK Magazine, explains. "And I think that when it didn't work out, it was heartbreaking for her. And I don't think she ever really dealt with it."

‌



And as their marriage collapsed in November 2006, Britney's 14 months of torment began. Just two months following the arrival of their second son, Jayden James, Britney initiated divorce proceedings, citing irreconcilable differences. Her increasingly unpredictable conduct saw her partying with Paris Hilton and Lindsay Lohan without underwear.

View 8 Images The popstar's marriage to Kevin Federline collapsed in November 2006

The passing of her cherished aunt, Sandra Covington, is believed to have triggered her downward spiral in January 2007. She was admitted to Eric Clapton's rehabilitation facility, Crossroads, in Antigua, but immediately returned to America and went to Kevin's residence, where he denied her access to their children.

‌



Steve Dennis, author of Britney - Inside The Dream, suggests that by this stage she was no longer "thinking straight". "Britney is in chaos. She's in turmoil, and she's in massive anxiety. And what you see, what we all see, sadly, is Britney acting out, lashing out, rebelling, losing control," he says. What followed shocked the entire world.

Furious, exasperated and encircled by 70 or more paparazzi, Britney drove to a local hair salon and requested hairdresser Esther Tognozz to shave her head. Tognozz declined and attempted to dissuade her, but the moment her back was turned, Britney grabbed the clippers and cut off half her hair herself.

View 8 Images After her relationship breakdown, Britney spent time partying with Hollywood It girls such as Paris Hilton

‌



"She had two bodyguards supposedly keeping an eye out to make sure the paparazzi were not getting any pictures," Tognozz remembers as footage reveals her minders laughing with photographers. "They were allowing the paparazzi to take her picture," says Rob Shuter, suggesting they may even have been paid for it. The most heartbreaking moment, however, was witnessing Britney's expression transform from a smile to one of panic and distress as she grasped what she had done.

Visibly dejected, she worried that her "mum was going to be p***ed," before composing herself and making her way to the Body and Soul Tattoo studio. Breaking her silence for the first time, tattooist Emily Wynne Hughes remembered hearing "an insane roaring sound outside" as Britney arrived with the paparazzi in tow.

"I wasn't sure what was happening, if there was a riot outside, and then the flashes came. The door opened slowly, and a hooded figure walked in," she recalled. Britney initially requested a tattoo of another woman's lips, followed by a small cross. When Wynne Hughes enquired about what had happened to the star's hair, she was startled by her unusual response.

‌



View 8 Images The troubled singer shaved off her own trademark hair, reportedly declaring, 'I'm sick of people touching my hair' (Image: X17Online.com)

"It was, you know, 'I just don't want anybody, anybody touching my head. I don't want anyone touching my hair. I'm sick of people touching my hair," she recalled. Rather than supporting their high-profile client, the tattoo artist noted that the troubled star's entourage appeared entirely "disengaged".

"It just didn't seem like they cared very much. I've felt this insane sense of anxiety and energy that felt so negative," she explained. "It just seemed like everybody was waiting for the crash to happen." The next day, Britney persisted in dismantling her carefully crafted image, adopting peculiar accents and donning various wigs. Still dressed in the previous evening's attire, she attempted to book into the luxury Mondrian hotel in Los Angeles without any money or credit cards - merely some figures jotted down on paper.

‌



After staff turned her away, she requested access to the swimming pool and wandered about in her undergarments before proceeding to shave her legs at the basin in the changing facilities. Other hotel patrons remembered her approaching strangers, asking for friendship whilst consuming multiple alcoholic beverages.

View 8 Images (Image: X17Online.com)

Rolling Stone reported she remained awake for 48 consecutive hours, convinced she was being pursued by demons or that a mobile phone charger was recording her innermost thoughts. Four days on, she returned to rehabilitation. Once more, she departed prematurely and headed directly to Kevin's residence, and, once more, he denied her access to the children.

‌



And when her cousin, Alli Sims, transported her to a local petrol station, it was at that location where she finally lost control and assaulted a photographer and his vehicle with an umbrella. Photographer Dano Ramos recounts in the documentary: "She went after us and attacked my truck," adding, "She was grinding her teeth and just beaten my truck up and she started going after [fellow photographer] Roberto, he turns his back, and she swipes him in the back with the umbrella."

Author Steve Dennis suggests her explosive reaction was characteristic of most people when "something just becomes crowded and claustrophobic. "You lash out. And what was in her hand at that time was an umbrella. And she was lashing out not just at the paparazzi, but at the press and at fame and who she had become and the life that she could never get back," he explains.

View 8 Images Losing control at a petrol station, Britney assaulted a photographer and his vehicle with an umbrella (Image: X17Online.com)

‌



On this occasion, she attended Promises rehab in Malibu and successfully completed her month-long programme. However, her greatest fear became reality when the courts awarded Kevin full custody in October 2007, after he alleged Britney had a habitual, frequent and continuous use of controlled substances and alcohol.

Devastated and with nobody she could rely on, she purchased two baby blue parakeets and named them Sean and Jayden after her sons. "That's how sad she was. She just needed companionship," Hollywood gossip journalist Janet Charlton observes. Despite Britney's deteriorating state, the media frenzy surrounding her only intensified, and following one court-supervised visit with her children, she barricaded herself in the bathroom with Jayden for three hours.

Police vehicles, fire engines and ambulances descended upon her mansion, and when the star was eventually persuaded to emerge, she was laughing hysterically as paramedics restrained her and secured her to a gurney. Upon arrival at the hospital, she was placed on a 5250 psychiatric hold, which permitted doctors to detain her for 14 days for her own protection.

‌



View 8 Images Kevin was awarded full custody of their sons in October 2007 (Image: X17Online)

Her parents intervened to assume control of her affairs, placing her under a conservatorship that remains in effect to this day. For her agent, Nancy Carson, who'd first encountered her as an angelic and pure eight-year-old, the incident proved both devastating and reassuring simultaneously.

"I cried. I just was so glad when her parents stepped in and did what they did, and they did exactly what they should do as parents," she revealed. "Yes, I cried. I loved this little girl, and I knew she was in pain, and it broke my heart."

Article continues below

For emotional support you can call the Samaritans 24-hour helpline on 116 123, email jo@samaritans.org, visit a Samaritans branch in person or go to the Samaritans website.

Do you have a story to share? Email me at julia.banim@reachplc.com. Follow Mirror Celebs on Snapchat , Instagram , Twitter , Facebook , YouTube and Threads

News.title: Keir Starmer vows to 'shield' Brits in Middle East from Iran's missiles and drones
News.content:
Keir Starmer vows to 'shield' Brits in Middle East from Iran's missiles and drones

The Prime Minister warned the conflict in the Middle East could continue 'for some time' - as he scrambled four more Typhoon jets to Qatar to bolster defences of the Gulf states

View 3 Images Keir Starmer appearing at No9 Downing Street (Image: POOL/AFP via Getty Images)

Keir Starmer has vowed to shield Brits and the UK's allies in the Middle East from Iran's onslaught of missiles and drones.

‌



The Prime Minister warned the war could continue "for some time" as he scrambled four more Typhoon jets to Qatar to bolster defences of the Gulf states. Two Wildcat helicopters armed with drone-busting missiles will arrive also arrive in Cyprus on Friday with the HMS Dragon warship also being deployed to the Mediterranean.

‌



Mr Starmer said this would help "take out Iranian missiles before they are fired at our people and our allies" with the conflict plunging the region into chaos. He said: "We will maintain this shield over British people in the region and our allies. We are working to strengthen it every day."

‌



Addressing the nation from a No9 Downing Street press conference, he said he wanted to reassure people who "are worried sick" about family and friends caught up in the conflict. He added: "As a nation, we should come together in this moment... we are united by our common humanity - and our love of this country. This conflict could continue for some time.

"So I want you to know that your government is resolute in our response – at home and abroad. We will do everything we can to protect British lives, uphold British values and safeguard the national interest."

7 things Keir Starmer announced in his live TV address to nation on Iran crisis READ MORE :

Keir Starmer LIVE: PM has not spoken to Trump since Saturday but insists special relationship alive READ MORE :

View 3 Images HMS Dragon warship is also being deployed to the Mediterranean (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



And he confirmed the first government evacuation flight had left Muscat, Oman, after take-off was cancelled on Wednesday evening due to a technical issue. After chairing an emergency Cobra meet, Mr Starmer said at 2.11pm the plane carrying around 180 British nationals left "a few minutes ago".

One passenger, who gave his name as Mark, said the planned route appeared to be from Muscat to London Stansted, with a refuelling stop in Cairo, Egypt. He told the BBC the situation on Wednesday night was a "complete shambles" involving "loads of issues with check-in" and "no communication from the craft or the crew" while being stuck on a transfer bus.

British Airways said it will run a fourth daily flight from Muscat to London Heathrow - a route it does not usually serve - departing at 10.30pm GMT on Saturday. It said in a statement: "We remain unable to operate flights from Abu Dhabi, Amman, Bahrain, Doha, Dubai and Tel Aviv."

‌



Mr Starmer said: "We will lay on additional charter flights in the coming days. British Airways is putting on daily flights from Oman, and we will keep working with all of our partners to increase the speed and capacity of this airlift. I want to be very clear, this is a huge undertaking. It's one of the biggest operations of its kind, many times bigger than the evacuation from Afghanistan. It's not going to happen overnight, but we will not stop until our people are safe."

More than 140,000 Britons have registered their presence in the Middle East with the Foreign Office, which set up a crisis-centre at the weekend when the conflict broke out.

View 3 Images Keir Starmer appearing at a press conference in Downing Street on Thursday (Image: PA)

‌



The PM also insisted he stood by his initial decision not to join US-Israeli strikes on Tehran at the weekend - which led to the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. He has since given permission for America to use British military bases for "defensive" strikes to protect countries being targeted by Tehran.

The initial decision not to grant the approval has led to bitter personal attacks on the PM by Donald Trump in recent days. Speaking at the Oval Office earlier this week, the US President said of Mr Starmer: "This is not Winston Churchill that we're dealing with."

But the PM insisted the UK-US special relationship was alive and "in operation right now" - despite having not spoken to Mr Trump directly since Saturday evening. He said: "Look, the special relationship is in operation right now. We're working with the Americans in the deployment from our bases.

Article continues below

"We are working together in the region, the US and the British, working together to protect both the US and the British in joint bases where we're jointly located, and we're sharing intelligence on a 24/7 basis in the usual way."

He added: "That is the special relationship. That is a special relationship in operation and, clearly, it's for the president to take decisions that he considers in the national interest, the right decisions, for the US. I understand that, I respect that, but equally, it's for me as the British Prime Minister to take decisions that I consider to be in the best interest of the United Kingdom. There's nothing controversial about that. The special relationship is operating right here at an important point."

News.title: Starmer tries to reassure as Iran crisis deepens
News.content: It was notable that this framed the decision entirely as a matter of policy, compared to his Commons statement at the start of this week in which he said not only that he did not believe there was a thought-through plan, but also strongly suggested he believed the strikes were illegal.

News.title: Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times, satellite image reveals
News.content: Iranian officials have blamed the US and Israel for the attack, however neither country has accepted responsibility. Israel says it was not aware of any operations in the area, while US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that Washington was still investigating the incident and that it would "never target, civilian targets".

News.title: How depleted weapons stockpiles could affect the Iran conflict
News.content: Patriot missiles have been in high demand – not just from the US but also its Arab allies and Ukraine. Each interceptor missile costs more than $4m (£3m) and the US is believed to currently produce around 700 a year. If Iran is still able to launch ballistic missiles, it will eat into these limited stockpiles.

News.title: Iran targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq
News.content: "It's not about the hours or days. We cannot move if the air above us is not cleaned. We need to see weapons depots [of Iran's security forces] being destroyed. Otherwise, it would be suicidal."

News.title: Failed asylum seeker families to be offered up to £40k to leave UK
News.content: Under changes due to take effect in June, the government will limit accommodation and support to "those who genuinely need it", although it is yet to set out in detail how this will work.

News.title: Covid inquiry chair defends £200m cost and four-year process on final day
News.content: "I hope that when they read about the extent of the suffering that we've heard and see the results of the inquiry's work, they will appreciate the huge scale of loss caused by Covid 19 and they will understand better why this inquiry was established."

News.title: UK firms pull fixed energy deals as Iran war pushes up prices
News.content: "We can no longer absorb the full cost of the energy we buy in advance for new fixed‑tariff customers if they choose to leave us during the period of the fix... Other suppliers have exit fees even in normal circumstances, but it's rare for Octopus," a spokesperson said.

News.title: Triple killer put flatmate in headlock in assault
News.content: In a short video clip shown to the inquiry, Calocane could be seen with his arm around the neck of his flatmate, holding him in a headlock while Christopher was heard asking for someone to "ring the police".

Boxing.title: Eddie Hearn claims Conor Benn offered him money to keep quiet over his controversial decision to defect to bitter rival Dana White's Zuffa Boxing
Boxing.content:
Eddie Hearn claims Conor Benn offered him money to keep quiet over his controversial decision to defect to bitter rival Dana White's Zuffa Boxing

Eddie Hearn has claimed that Conor Benn offered him money to avoid speaking negatively about their split after the British boxer signed a blockbuster deal with Zuffa Boxing.

Benn's decision to leave Hearn's Matchroom stable after a decade together came as a surprise within the industry, with the 29-year-old agreeing terms on what has been widely reported as a $15million one-fight deal with the new promotional venture headed up by Dana White.

Hearn, who guided Benn from his professional debut at 19 and oversaw his rise to world-level contention, admitted he was 'devastated' by the departure. He also revealed he was given just three days to match the offer - which he felt was 'completely unreasonable commercially'.

Despite Benn publicly insisting he would still like a relationship with his former promoter and even suggesting there could be a place for him within his team, Hearn says part of the exit discussions left him stunned and disappointed in his former star.

Speaking to iFL TV about the way in which Benn handled the situation, Hearn said: 'There was something else which was, "We'll give you a few quid if you don't say anything bad", kind of thing.

'And I was like, What? "We still want to work with you and here's a few quid".

Hearn has claimed Benn offered him money to avoid speaking negatively about their split

Benn announced he had jumped ship to Dana White's promotional company Zuffa Boxing

'And I'm just like… so I've received it and it's gone to Frank Smith and Shaun Palmer, who we're kind of the main operators within the business.

'And we're all then straight on like, "Have you seen that? Are they f***ing having a laugh?" Everyone was just completely shocked.'

Hearn also expressed frustration at how the communication was handled, revealing he first learned of developments formally through Benn's legal team rather than directly from the fighter.

He said: 'I text Conor maybe four or five days later and said, "I really think we should have a call. For everything that I've done for you, I don't think it's fair that I should have to deal with a letter from your lawyer. Let's just have a chat".

'Basically, man to man. And he said, "I heard you were quite emotional on the call. I think we should just let it all die down and talk another time".

'I'm like, "f*** me". And that was probably the moment where I just thought I cannot believe on the believe.'

While reiterating that he respects any fighter's right to maximise their earning potential, Hearn insisted his issue was not financial.

'To be honest, I think people get it a little bit mixed up between a fighter shouldn't be denied an opportunity to make a load of money for their family and all this kind of stuff,' he said.

Benn's decision to leave Hearn's Matchroom stable after a decade together came as a surprise

Conor Benn will fight Regis Prograis on April 11 on the same card as the returning Tyson Fury

'I couldn't agree more. This is not about that. This is just the way that you act to someone that has done so much for you and has basically saved your career.

'And if you listen to the Conor Benn story, I don't want to say saved his life, but the people who were there for him like that – which you can count on one hand – played a massive part in that.

'So to forget them or to treat them with disloyalty for me is unforgivable.'

Benn's next bout is scheduled for April 11 in a 150lb catchweight clash against former 140lb world champion Regis Prograis.



He is coming off two high-profile contests with Chris Eubank Jr and remains highly ranked at welterweight as he prepares to begin the next chapter of his career under new promotional leadership.

Boxing.title: Inside the incredible plans to transform the Pyramids of Giza into a heavyweight boxing venue for Oleksandr Usyk's historic fight
Boxing.content:
Oleksandr Usyk will swap packed stadiums for one of the most extraordinary settings sport has ever seen when he takes on former kickboxing champion Rico Verhoeven on May 23.

The two men are set to clash in front of the Pyramids of Giza with the WBC heavyweight world title on the line after Mauricio Sulaiman confirmed the green and gold belt will be at stake.

The WBC president has granted Usyk special permission to make a voluntary defence of his title before fulfilling his mandatory obligation, a bout against interim champion Agit Kabayel.

For Usyk, May's event will represent a slightly unconventional chapter in a career defined by taking on the biggest challenges in the most hostile environments.

Verhoeven, meanwhile, will dip his toe into boxing's ultimate proving ground and his first outing in a professional boxing ring for a decade after years of kickboxing dominance.

For organisers, it will be more than just a fight. It will be a global event, designed to merge elite combat sport with one of the most iconic backdrops on Earth.

Oleksandr Usyk will swap packed stadiums for one of the most extraordinary settings sport has ever seen when he takes on former kickboxing champion Rico Verhoeven on May 23

The two men are set to clash in front of the Pyramids of Giza (Verhoeven pictured above)

When Tiesto took to the decks on the Pyramids of Giza last December, the ancient plateau was transformed into something resembling a sci-fi movie set.

Lasers sliced through the sky, vast LED screens rose from the sand, and the pyramids themselves were illuminated like the backdrop to a Hollywood movie.

Yet for all the spectacle, what mattered most was what you couldn't see.

Behind the scenes, the entire operation ran like a military exercise. The stage was completely temporary - no digging, no drilling, no permanent fixings.

Every truss, lighting tower and screen was carefully weighted and balanced to ensure the desert surface (and the buried archaeology beneath it) remained untouched.

It's believed a fight involving Usyk would follow exactly the same blueprint: a raised, freestanding ring platform constructed entirely above ground and dismantled within hours of the final bell.

One obvious concern is noise. Heavyweight boxing is not exactly subtle.

But during Tiesto's show, engineers developed a 'heritage-safe' sound system that directed bass frequencies away from the pyramids.

When Tiesto took to the decks on the Pyramids of Giza last December, the ancient plateau was transformed into something resembling a sci-fi movie set

The WBC president has granted Usyk special permission to make a voluntary defence before fulfilling his mandatory obligation, a bout that must come against Agit Kabayel

For Usyk, May's event will represent a slightly unconventional chapter in a career defined by taking on the biggest challenges in the most hostile environments

Real-time vibration sensors monitored the limestone structures throughout the night to ensure sound levels stayed within strict archaeological limits.

A boxing fight would produce nothing like the sustained low-frequency rumble of a three-hour electronic set. Even so, vibration monitoring would almost certainly be in place again as an added safeguard.

The logistics would be just as tightly controlled. For Tiesto's event, guests entered via the Grand Egyptian Museum before being transported by dedicated shuttle to the secured concert zone on the plateau. A fight night would be expected to follow a similarly high-end access plan.

The concert footprint included a vast main arena space, VIP table sections, hospitality and brand activation zones, plus food and beverage areas spread across the sand.

For boxing, that layout could easily translate into a ringside enclosure carved into the desert, elevated hospitality decks and dramatic broadcast lighting tailored for a global pay-per-view audience.

Security, too, was uncompromising. Mandatory bag checks, controlled access points and a strict no re-entry policy were enforced throughout the concert - measures that would likely be even tighter for a world-title bout staged in the shadow of the last surviving wonder of the ancient world.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua takes a shot at Conor Benn in Instagram post as Brit shows his support for long-time promoter Eddie Hearn after former stablemate left Matchroom for rival Zuffa Boxing
Boxing.content:
Anthony Joshua has waded into the ongoing feud between Conor Benn and Eddie Hearn, lending his support to the promoter.

Benn, son of British legend Nigel, sensationally announced last month that he was ditching Hearn's promotional team Matchroom Boxing after a decade-long relationship - leaving for his rival Dana White's outfit Zuffa Boxing instead.

Hearn has since spoken out about that decision, revealing he was left 'devastated' by Benn having guided the British boxer from his 2016 debut to headlining cards and sticking by him after failing two drug tests in 2022.

And on Tuesday another long-term Matchroom Boxing fighter in Joshua showed his support to Hearn.

Uploading to his Instagram story, the former two-time heavyweight world champion shared a picture of him and Hearn dressed sharply.

Accompanied with that was a caption which read: 'AJ: Did you bring the fire power Eddie?

Anthony Joshua (left) took to Instagram on Tuesday to lend his support for Eddie Hearn (right) after Conor Benn left his Matchroom Boxing for rivals Zuffa Boxing last month

Benn (top) stunned Hearn when he decided to end their 10-year working relationship

'Eddie: It's tucked on my waste line mush [sic]

'AJ: I'll let you know when I have a clear sight of Connor Benn [sic]'.

When it was announced that he was splitting from Hearn, Benn - who has ambitions of winning the WBC welterweight title - thanked the 46-year-old for standing by him during the tough periods of his career and said he was excited for a 'new chapter' aimed at securing 'legacy fights' on the biggest stages.

However, speaking to iFL TV after Benn's bombshell announcement, Hearn claimed he was informed of the decision by the boxer's lawyer before being refused a phone call with him.

'Me personally, pretty devastated,' Hearn said of his reaction to the news. 'I have had a few days to digest what might happen. It is not often you get shocked.

'I blame myself. I forgot it was boxing. I just felt the loyalty that we have shown would never ever put us in this position.'

Daily Mail Sport reported that Hearn is thought to have lent Benn £500,000 following the collapse of his 2022 match-up with Eubank Jr, which came about due to his positive tests.

Hearn added that he 'misjudged the character' of Benn by feeling the 'loyalty' he had shown him 'would be enough to talk it through or get close to a number'.

He continued: 'It feels a little bit of a wasted few years. I gave a lot... I believed him and I backed him and I never gave in when he was done, out. Loaned him hundreds of thousands of pounds, but yeah on we go.'

'I just think the way it was done was very very wrong,' he added. 'But everyone is different. Some people, loyalty really matters, other people maybe not so.

'The way it played out to receive an email from the lawyer and then to ask for a call and not the willingness to have it, you just lose a bit of your soul to it.'

Benn's first bout as a Zuffa Boxing fighter will be against Regis Prograis on April 11 at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.

Boxing.title: Aussie boxer reveals that he received shocking death threats following the controversial ending to his fight with Nikita Tszyu
Boxing.content:
Aussie boxer Michael Zerafa has revealed he received multiple death threats after the controversial ending to his fight against Nikita Tszyu, adding that the online reaction ‘broke’ him.

The 33-year-old threatened to walk away from boxing following the no-contest, after he was booed out of the Brisbane Entertainment Centre in January, but has since revealed he is eager to return to challenge some of the biggest names in the sport.

Fans were left fuming after his fight against Tszyu in January ended prematurely when Zerafa had told a ringside doctor that he couldn’t see out of his left eye after accidentally clashing heads with his opponent.

Later, during a post-fight interview, Zerafa said that the ‘doctor had called it off’. The saga then took another controversial twist, with the Aussie boxer stating that his vision was just ‘blurry’, before adding that he wanted to continue fighting.

Spectators inside the Brisbane Entertainment Centre booed the Australian while some threw cans towards the ring.

Michael Zerafa (pictured) has revealed that he received 20 death threats in the 48 hours following his controversial fight with NikitaTszyu

The bout was called a no-contest after Zerafa told a ringside doctor that he couldn't see out of his left eye after clashing heads with Tszyu (left)

Tszyu has since categorically ruled out a rematch.

‘I'm closing the book on it. I'm just disappointed for everybody involved. I'm gutted for everyone,’ Tszyu said.

After the fight, Zerafa claimed in a social media post that he had suffered a shattered eye socket and damage to his retina. He had spent time in a Brisbane hospital, but his team did not disclose the doctor’s findings.

The 33-year-old has now spoken for the first time since the bout, but did not comment on the moment where he declared that he couldn’t see.

Speaking to The BillyJoe Podcast, he said: ‘Still to this day I don’t understand [the hate].

‘People say: “It’s because you b****ed it”, but I didn’t.

‘I had a genuine injury. I was winning the fight.’

After the bout, Zerafa issued an apology to fans, claiming that his choice of words was ‘poor’.

Zerafa had revealed that he had suffered a shattered eye-socket on social media following the fight

Zerafa (left) revealed that the criticism in the aftermath of the bout 'broke' him

He also doubled down on the legitimacy of his injury, stating that his vision was only 6/20. He added that ‘there was an issue with my eyesight, but it does not change the fact that my wording was poor.’

That apology has since been removed from his Instagram.

In the immediate aftermath of the bout, fans took to social media to send him abusive messages, with Zerafa revealing that some had even gone to his sponsors to ask why they were partnering with the Australian.

‘It broke me. I was on top. I’ve given my absolute all for this,’ Zerafa told the podcast. ‘When all that subsided, to see death threats, memes…

‘In that 48 hours, I reckon I copped 20 [death threats],’ he added.

‘We’re talking 48 hours. You get so much crap, man.

‘All directed at me. For what?’

He was pressed further on what kind of threats he received.

‘It was: “When I see you, I’ll make sure I’ll cut your throat, I know where you train, be expecting me there”.’

‘They reach out to sponsors and say: “As if you’re sponsoring this guy”.

‘Sometimes it makes me just want to slap these people. How much do you hate your life?

‘I get you might be jealous, but go and be jealous quietly.’

Questions have mounted over his future in the sport, with Zerafa’s relationship with No Limit Boxing said to have deteriorated.

But the 33-year-old, who holds a 34-5-0 record, added that he is rebuilding and is determined to challenge for a world title.

While he has kept a very low profile over the past two months, the fighter took to Instagram over the weekend to publish a video announcing that he was working hard to get back to competing.

He even shot a dig at those who had criticised him, writing in the caption of the video: 'And yeah... I can see now.'

Zerafa has now announced that he is hoping to get back to the ring and challenge the best in the game, poking a dig at his critics by writing on Instagram: 'And yeah... I can see now'

‘I’m not done yet. I know a lot of people would like to see me done, but I’ve still got a fire burning,’ Zerafa said during the podcast, when asked about what the future holds.

‘I’ve got a lot of people to prove wrong, I’m hungrier and better things are coming.

‘I’ve cleaned out my backyard, it’s time to get back to where I belong, back with the bigger guys, back where people know what they’re doing.

‘Boxing is a beautiful sport, but there’s too many people with their hands in it who are destroying it.

‘And I don’t even need to say names.’

Boxing.title: Revealed: Anthony Mundine's startling courtroom act as he has a bizarre clash with judge
Boxing.content:
Anthony Mundine has left a Sydney courtroom stunned after performing an acknowledgement of country and refusing to follow standard plea procedure during a tense exchange with a magistrate.

The former rugby league star and world champion boxer appeared at Sutherland Local Court last Tuesday, where he represented himself on a charge of driving during a disqualification period.

Before proceedings began, Mundine performed an acknowledgement of country and addressed the court in unusual terms.

'I'm Anthony of the tribe Mundine, here in my private capacity,' he said.

He also acknowledged the 'Dharug people of the Eora Nation'.

The court was then drawn into a procedural standoff when Mundine declined to enter a formal plea to the charge.

Anthony Mundine caused a scene when he appeared in a Sydney court over a charge of driving during a disqualification period

The former NRL and boxing star conducted an acknowledgement of country and refused to enter a plea to the charge

Magistrate Jillian Kiely explained that the legal process required a clear plea of either guilty or not guilty.

'If you fail to make a plea, I can't make a plea of not guilty,' the magistrate said.

Mundine refused to comply with the court's direction.

'I don't consent … I'm innocent,' he said.

Because Mundine would not formally enter a plea, the magistrate entered a not guilty plea on his behalf in accordance with court procedure.

The 50-year-old also attempted to have the charge dismissed, but the magistrate informed him that she did not have the authority to withdraw the matter. Only police could make that decision.

'They [police] will be held accountable … I'm joking,' he said.
